meta.title: NEXUS — Institutional Memory Engine
meta.description: Companies lose 42% of their knowledge when senior employees leave. NEXUS is a RAG system that detects contradictions in your documents and grounds every answer in its sources.

hero.titlePost: of their knowledge when senior employees leave.
hero.sub: NEXUS turns your documents into an institution that remembers — answering with cited sources, grounding every claim in the evidence, and catching the moment two documents disagree.
hero.wakingNote: First visit in a while — the free server is waking up (~30–60s). This only happens once.
hero.errorRetry: The demo server is taking a moment — retry →

pipeline.lead: Every query runs through four stages — and every stage is auditable.
pipeline.step1Desc: PDF, DOCX, TXT parsed via Unstructured, split into 800-token semantic chunks with overlap and section headers.
pipeline.step2Desc: Dense pgvector search and sparse BM25 run in parallel, then fuse with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) — beating vector-only on keyword-heavy queries.
pipeline.step3Desc: Gemini 2.0 Flash streams the answer token-by-token over an LCEL chain — context wrapped in document tags to neutralise prompt injection. Natively bilingual: English and French.
pipeline.step4Desc: Answer grounding checks every claim against the cited sources, plus a second LLM pass that compares them — surfacing contradictions and knowledge gaps before you trust the answer.

features.title: Beyond chat-with-docs
features.lead: Four capabilities that make answers trustworthy — not just fluent.
features.item1Desc: A second structured LLM pass compares retrieved chunks across documents and flags conflicting statements with a severity rating.
features.item2Desc: Flags when the corpus has no real answer instead of hallucinating one — telling you what your documents are missing.
features.item3Desc: Every answer cites its exact document, page, and excerpt — expandable inline, never a black box.
features.item4Desc: Checks how much of each answer is backed by the cited sources — a transparent coverage score, not an opaque confidence number.

showcase.titlePre: It tells you when your own docs
showcase.lead: A real exchange from the demo corpus — two HR policies, one year apart.
showcase.question: What is the remote work policy? How many days per week?
showcase.answerPre: According to the 2024 HR Policy, employees may work remotely up to
showcase.contradictionDesc: These two policies disagree on the remote-work limit. The 2024 version appears more recent and may supersede the 2023 policy.

cta.sub: One click loads a live corpus with built-in contradictions. No signup.

footer.tagline: Built by Oussama Skia (SKAY) · RAG · hybrid retrieval · contradiction detection · MIT licensed

chatWindow.askOrPick: Ask anything — or pick a question below
chatWindow.eachQuestion: Each question showcases a different NEXUS capability. Watch for the contradiction badge, grounding panel, and source cards.
chatWindow.sessionLimit: Session limit reached (20 queries). Refresh to start a new session.
chatWindow.s1Question: What is the remote work policy and how many days per week are allowed?
chatWindow.s2Question: What was the Q3 2023 marketing budget?
chatWindow.s3Question: What is the current status of Project Atlas?
chatWindow.s4Question: What are the key product priorities in the 2024 roadmap?
chatWindow.uploadDesc: Drop your files into the panel above — or click "Upload documents" in the top bar. PDF, DOCX, TXT and MD are supported.
chatWindow.workspaceReadyDesc: {count, plural, one {# document indexed} other {# documents indexed}} — ask your first question.
chatWindow.workspaceS1Question: What are the main topics covered across my documents?
chatWindow.workspaceS2Question: Are there any contradictions or conflicting information in my documents?
chatWindow.workspaceS3Question: Give me a concise summary of all my documents.

grounding.singleSource: Based on a single document source.

sidebar.contradictionMsg: {count, plural, one {# contradiction detected.} other {# contradictions detected.}} Review flagged responses.

documentZone.formats: PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD — max 20 MB · multiple files supported
documentZone.loaded: {count, plural, one {# document loaded} other {# documents loaded}}
